---
title: "Sanity checks"
author: "Jirka Vomlel"
date: "2026-03-25"
output: html_document
---

```{r setup, include=FALSE}
knitr::opts_chunk$set(echo = TRUE)
```

## R Markdown

This is an R Markdown document. Markdown is a simple formatting syntax for authoring HTML, PDF, and MS Word documents. For more details on using R Markdown see <http://rmarkdown.rstudio.com>.

When you click the **Knit** button a document will be generated that includes both content as well as the output of any embedded R code chunks within the document. You can embed an R code chunk like this:

```{r}
library(mvtnorm)

# --- Define a known PPCA model ---
set.seed(123)
n     <- 1000   # large n so ML estimates are close to truth
p     <- 6
k     <- 2

mu     <- rnorm(p)
sigma2 <- 0.5

# Random loading matrix W (p x k)
W <- matrix(rnorm(p * k), nrow = p, ncol = k)

# --- Generate data from the true model ---
Z <- matrix(rnorm(n * k), nrow = n)          # latent scores ~ N(0, I_k)
E <- matrix(rnorm(n * p, sd = sqrt(sigma2)), nrow = n)  # noise
X_group <- sweep(Z %*% t(W) + E, 2, mu, "+") # n x p data matrix

# --- Ground truth log-likelihood via implied multivariate normal ---
C        <- W %*% t(W) + sigma2 * diag(p)
ll_exact <- sum(dmvnorm(X_group, mean = mu, sigma = C, log = TRUE))

# --- PPCA log-likelihood at ML estimates ---
ll_ppca <- ppca_loglik(X_group, k)

cat(sprintf("True model log-likelihood : %.3f\n", ll_exact))
cat(sprintf("PPCA ML log-likelihood    : %.3f\n", ll_ppca))
cat(sprintf("Difference (should be small for large n): %.3f\n", ll_ppca - ll_exact))
stopifnot(ll_ppca >= ll_exact)  # ML estimates cannot do worse than true params
```

```{r}
X_test <- matrix(rnorm(300 * 8), nrow = 300)
lls <- sapply(1:7, function(k) ppca_loglik(X_test, k))
stopifnot(all(diff(lls) > 0))
```

```{r}
# Assumes W, sigma2, mu, p, k (=2), X_group are still in the workspace from check 1

# Compute PPCA log-likelihood for all candidate k values
lls_structured <- sapply(1:(p - 1), function(k_try) ppca_loglik(X_group, k_try))
diffs          <- diff(lls_structured)

cat("Log-likelihoods by k:\n")
for (k_try in 1:(p - 1)) {
  cat(sprintf("  k = %d:  loglik = %10.3f  gain over k-1 = %s\n",
              k_try,
              lls_structured[k_try],
              if (k_try == 1) "       -" else sprintf("%8.3f", diffs[k_try - 1])))
}

cat(sprintf("\nTrue k* = %d\n", k))
cat("Gains should be large for k <= k* and drop sharply afterwards.\n")
```
```{r}
plot(1:(p-1), lls_structured, type = "b", pch = 19,
     xlab = "k (number of retained PCs)",
     ylab = "PPCA log-likelihood",
     main = sprintf("PPCA log-likelihood vs k  (true k* = %d)", k))
abline(v = k, lty = 2, col = "red")
legend("bottomright", legend = "true k*", lty = 2, col = "red")
```

=============================================================================
Check 5: BN parameter count recovery
=============================================================================
 A Gaussian BN with structure [A][B|A] has exactly 5 free parameters:
  Node A ~ N(mu_A, sigma2_A)          -> 2 params: mu_A, sigma2_A
  Node B|A ~ N(a + b*A, sigma2_B)     -> 3 params: a, b, sigma2_B
  Total = 5


```{r}
set.seed(42)
n_check5 <- 500
A  <- rnorm(n_check5)
B  <- 1.5 + 0.8 * A + rnorm(n_check5, sd = 0.5)
df_check5 <- data.frame(A = A, B = B)

dag_check5 <- model2network("[A][B|A]")
fit_check5 <- bn.fit(dag_check5, df_check5)

ll_check5  <- as.numeric(logLik(fit_check5, data = df_check5))
bic_check5 <- score(dag_check5, data = df_check5, type = "bic-g")

bn_nparams_check5 <- round(2 * (ll_check5 - bic_check5) / log(n_check5))

cat(sprintf("Recovered BN nparams : %d  (expected: 5)\n", bn_nparams_check5))
stopifnot(bn_nparams_check5 == 5)
cat("Check 5 passed.\n")
```
=============================================================================
Check 6: combined BIC recovers the correct granularity on synthetic data
=============================================================================
We generate data from a known PPCA + BN model with:
  Group 1: p=5 variables, true k*=2
  Group 2: p=4 variables, true k*=1
  Group 3: p=4 variables, true k*=2
with a simple BN structure G1_PC1 -> G2_PC1 -> G3_PC1 on the latent scores

```{r}
set.seed(99)
n6  <- 500
k_true <- c(2, 1, 2)   # true granularity we want to recover
p_grps <- c(5, 4, 4)   # group sizes

sigma2_grps <- c(0.3, 0.4, 0.3)   # PPCA noise per group

# --- Simulate latent BN scores ---
# Simple chain: Z1_1 -> Z2_1 -> Z3_1, others independent
Z1 <- cbind(rnorm(n6), rnorm(n6))                          # G1: 2 latent dims
Z2 <- cbind(0.7 * Z1[,1] + rnorm(n6, sd=0.5))             # G2: 1 latent dim
Z3 <- cbind(0.6 * Z2[,1] + rnorm(n6, sd=0.5), rnorm(n6)) # G3: 2 latent dims

# --- Generate observed variables via PPCA ---
make_group_data <- function(Z, p, sigma2) {
  k  <- ncol(Z)
  W  <- matrix(rnorm(p * k), nrow = p)
  mu <- rnorm(p)
  E  <- matrix(rnorm(n6 * p, sd = sqrt(sigma2)), nrow = n6)
  sweep(Z %*% t(W) + E, 2, mu, "+")
}

X1 <- make_group_data(Z1, p_grps[1], sigma2_grps[1])
X2 <- make_group_data(Z2, p_grps[2], sigma2_grps[2])
X3 <- make_group_data(Z3, p_grps[3], sigma2_grps[3])

data6  <- cbind(X1, X2, X3)
groups6 <- list(1:5, 6:9, 10:13)

# --- Granularity search ---
candidates6 <- list(G1 = 1:4, G2 = 1:3, G3 = 1:3)
grid6       <- expand.grid(candidates6)

results6 <- do.call(rbind, lapply(seq_len(nrow(grid6)), function(i) {
  k_vec <- as.integer(grid6[i, ])
  res   <- learn_bn_pca_bic(data6, groups6, k_vec)
  data.frame(G1_npc = k_vec[1], G2_npc = k_vec[2], G3_npc = k_vec[3],
             bic_combined = res$bic_combined)
}))

results6 <- results6[order(-results6$bic_combined), ]

cat("\nCheck 6: Granularity search results (top 8):\n")
print(head(results6, 8), row.names = FALSE)

best6 <- results6[1, ]
cat(sprintf("\nBest found   : G1=%d, G2=%d, G3=%d\n",
            best6$G1_npc, best6$G2_npc, best6$G3_npc))
cat(sprintf("True k*      : G1=%d, G2=%d, G3=%d\n",
            k_true[1], k_true[2], k_true[3]))

if (all(as.integer(best6[1:3]) == k_true)) {
  cat("Check 6 passed: combined BIC recovered the true granularity.\n")
} else {
  cat("Check 6: best config differs from true k* -- inspect top results.\n")
}
```

```{r}
# Re-run check 6 with larger n to see if true k* is recovered asymptotically
for (n_try in c(500, 1000, 2000, 5000, 10000, 20000)) {
  set.seed(99)
  
  Z1 <- cbind(rnorm(n_try), rnorm(n_try))
  Z2 <- cbind(0.7 * Z1[,1] + rnorm(n_try, sd = 0.5))
  Z3 <- cbind(0.6 * Z2[,1] + rnorm(n_try, sd = 0.5), rnorm(n_try))
  
  make_group_data_n <- function(Z, p, sigma2, n) {
    k  <- ncol(Z)
    W  <- matrix(rnorm(p * k), nrow = p)
    mu <- rnorm(p)
    E  <- matrix(rnorm(n * p, sd = sqrt(sigma2)), nrow = n)
    sweep(Z %*% t(W) + E, 2, mu, "+")
  }
  
  X1 <- make_group_data_n(Z1, 5, 0.3, n_try)
  X2 <- make_group_data_n(Z2, 4, 0.4, n_try)
  X3 <- make_group_data_n(Z3, 4, 0.3, n_try)
  
  data_try   <- cbind(X1, X2, X3)
  grid_try   <- expand.grid(list(G1=1:4, G2=1:3, G3=1:3))
  
  results_try <- do.call(rbind, lapply(seq_len(nrow(grid_try)), function(i) {
    k_vec <- as.integer(grid_try[i,])
    res   <- learn_bn_pca_bic(data_try, groups6, k_vec)
    data.frame(G1=k_vec[1], G2=k_vec[2], G3=k_vec[3], bic=res$bic_combined)
  }))
  
  best_try <- results_try[which.max(results_try$bic), ]
  cat(sprintf("n=%5d: best = G1=%d, G2=%d, G3=%d\n",
              n_try, best_try$G1, best_try$G2, best_try$G3))
}
```
